evals.name: vague-caching-request
evals.prompt: I want to add caching to the CLI
evals.expected_output: Should ask structured clarifying questions (what problem, current state, scope, constraints) before proposing any design. Should offer multiple-choice options. Should summarize understanding before moving to approaches.

evals.name: detailed-webhook-request
evals.prompt: I need to add webhook support to maestro so that when a task completes, it can POST a JSON payload to a configured URL. The payload should include task ID, status, duration, and feature name. It should support retries with exponential backoff. Config lives in .maestro/config.json. Only HTTP POST, no other methods.
evals.expected_output: Should recognize that most questions are already answered. Should skip problem space and scope questions. Should focus on remaining gaps: error handling, retry limits, auth/headers, testing approach. Should move to design faster than the vague prompt.

evals.name: simple-flag-addition
evals.prompt: Add a --verbose flag to the status command that shows full task descriptions instead of truncated ones
evals.expected_output: Should recognize this is a simple, well-understood change. Should ask minimal questions (maybe 1-2 about output format). Should not run a full 10-question interview. Should move to design quickly.
